summary: The plan is close, but there is one significant coverage gap in provider placement that can leave some unit-formatted UI outside context and stuck on imperial.

issues.title: Wrapping only `children` can miss AppShell-owned consumers
issues.description: Step 6 specifies wrapping `children` with `UnitPreferenceProvider` in both `(app)` layout and `AuthAwareShell`. In this codebase, `AppShell` renders some consumers (notably panel content paths using `ProductDetailContent`) outside the `children` slot. If the provider only wraps `children`, those consumers fall back to the hook default (`"imperial"`) and ignore the saved preference.
issues.suggestion: Make Step 6 explicit that the provider must wrap the entire shell output tree (e.g., provider outside `AppShell` and outside the anonymous shell), not just `children`. Add a verification check for panel-based product detail rendering in the selected unit.
